meta.footEn: Data snapshot: 2026-07-06 · Garmin fenix 8 via Freddy

en.c5d: computed from sleep, HRV, RHR, stress & recent load
en.rec: Strong recovery signal: 7h20 at score 86 off a 22:39 bedtime — earlier than the weekend's late nights. The standout is the autonomic picture: HRV jumped to 51 (+10 over the previous night and well above the 7-day baseline of 44), RHR eased to 52 at the week's low, and morning stress is a very calm 12 after yesterday's 27. The one caveat is deep sleep — just 46 minutes (10% of the night) versus 1.5–1.9h on recent nights — but REM was strong at 1h54 and total time was ample, so the score held at 86. Nights are labeled by the evening they start, so this one sits under Sun 5 (Sunday–Monday); Thursday's night (Jul 2) is still the untracked slot on the chart. Yesterday was an easy Zagreb hike, so the tank is full heading into today's planned pool swim intervals.

hr.c5d: izračunato iz sna, HRV-a, RHR-a, stresa i opterećenja
hr.rec: Snažan signal oporavka: 7h20 uz ocjenu 86 i lijeganje u 22:39 — ranije od vikendskih kasnih noći. Ističe se autonomna slika: HRV je skočio na 51 (+10 u odnosu na prethodnu noć i znatno iznad 7-dnevne osnovice 44), RHR se spustio na 52, tjedni minimum, a jutarnji stres vrlo miran 12 nakon jučerašnjih 27. Jedina zamjerka je duboki san — samo 46 minuta (10% noći) naspram 1,5–1,9 h prethodnih noći — no REM je bio snažan 1h54, a ukupno vrijeme obilno, pa je ocjena ostala na 86. Noći se bilježe prema večeri kojom počinju, pa ova stoji pod Ned 5 (nedjelja–ponedjeljak); četvrtkova noć (2.7.) i dalje je nezabilježeni termin na grafu. Jučer je bila lagana planinarska tura u Zagrebu, pa je spremnik pun uoči današnjih planiranih bazenskih intervala.
